authoritative_sources.title: Ontology for Media Creation Part 1 Overview v2.7
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: MovieLabs is the studio-backed industry consortium behind the 2030 Vision, and this ontology is a direct workflow reference for production identifiers, relationships, and metadata lineage.

authoritative_sources.title: Specifications & Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DPP is a recognized media-supply-chain body whose published specifications and guidance are used by broadcasters, platforms, and suppliers to define delivery, metadata, and interchange expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Delivery baseline and metadata handoff setup

authoritative_sources.title: AS-11 X9: NABA HD AVC Contribution Specification
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AMWA publishes the formal AS-11 specification family used for air-ready program contribution workflows; X9 is directly tied to North American broadcaster delivery expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: DPP launches Live Production Exchange for Live Broadcasters
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This DPP publication documents the LPX framework for live broadcasters and ties live-production metadata exchange to AMWA NMOS-based workflows, which is directly relevant to live production analytics.

authoritative_sources.title: NewsML-G2 quick start guides
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IPTC is the recognized newsroom metadata standards body and NewsML-G2 is a direct operational reference for planning, package, and item metadata exchange in news production workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: SMPTE ST 12-1:2014 Time and Control Code
authoritative_sources.publisher: Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SMPTE ST 12-1 is the canonical timecode standard used to align shot, edit, logging, delivery, and playout evidence across media-production systems.

authoritative_sources.title: SMPTE ST 2067-3:2020 Interoperable Master Format - Composition Playlist
authoritative_sources.publisher: Society of Motion Picture and Television Engineers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SMPTE ST 2067-3 is the formal IMF composition-playlist standard and directly governs versioning, playback timelines, and external track-file references for file-based media mastering.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Standards for Media Rating Research
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The MRC is the recognized standards and accreditation body for media measurement, and its minimum standards define methodology disclosure, system-control, and reporting expectations for media ratings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience evidence qualification and disclosure review

authoritative_sources.title: MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards (Phase I Video)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: These MRC standards are a direct public baseline for cross-platform video audience measurement, comparability, and disclosure discipline across linear and streaming environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-platform audience trend and benchmark review

workflow_stages.name: Source qualification and production-context setup
workflow_stages.description: Identify the title, season or episode, version, release window, territory, platform, delivery target, and reporting purpose that govern the analysis before any metric is selected.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Metric-definition and artifact-lineage mapping
workflow_stages.description: Determine which schedule, cost, turnover, delivery, availability, and audience definitions apply and map each metric to named production, post, delivery, and measurement artifacts.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Cross-system reconciliation and exception review
workflow_stages.description: Compare scheduling, production-accounting, asset-management, delivery, playout, and audience records to identify matched metrics, unexplained variances, unsupported acceptance states, and source-of-truth conflicts.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Analysis and disclosure package assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble analytical briefs, dashboards, or disclosure-oriented summaries with explicit caveats, source lineage, refresh assumptions, methodology labeling, and benchmark limitations.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Boundary and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the work requires budget authority, payroll or residual signoff, delivery acceptance, rights clearance, accessibility signoff, labor or safety interpretation, or public-disclosure approval.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: stripboards, call sheets, daily production reports, unit reports, and location summaries
hot-cost snapshots, budget workpapers, purchase-order summaries, and approved budget baselines
turnover lists, edit decision lists, timecode references, asset manifests, and MAM exports
IMF package manifests, AS-11 or equivalent delivery packages, broadcaster or platform acceptance logs, and QC defect reports
caption, subtitle, and accessibility artifact sets plus exception records
broadcast as-run logs, newsroom rundown metadata, streaming CMS availability reports, and publish logs
audience-measurement packs, first-party telemetry extracts, benchmark decks, and disclosure review notes
data warehouse outputs, BI dashboards, and source-precedence policy documents
systems_of_record: media asset management and digital asset management platforms
edit, finishing, and post-production tracking systems
delivery, QC, and accessibility management systems
broadcast traffic, automation, and newsroom systems
streaming CMS, availability, and platform operations systems
data warehouse, business-intelligence, and document repository systems

allowed: Define production metrics and source hierarchies when the title, version, window, and reporting purpose are explicit.
Assemble evidence-backed analytical briefs, caveats, reconciliation notes, and disclosure-risk summaries.
Separate schedule, cost, post, delivery, availability, and audience record paths when the public baseline requires different evidence or definitions.
escalate: Commissioner delivery specifications, rights windows, incident holds, or audience methodology are missing or conflict with the public baseline.
Production trackers, post systems, delivery dashboards, and audience records materially disagree on a publishable metric.
A requested claim requires accounting signoff, delivery approval, accessibility signoff, labor or safety interpretation, rights clearance, or disclosure approval.
refuse: Requests to approve budget changes, certify payroll or residual treatment, sign off delivery acceptance, or waive rights or compliance obligations.
Requests to treat delivered, published, available, watched, completed, and benchmark-ready as interchangeable without mapped definitions.
Requests that use the media-production industry label as cosmetic and skip title, version, timecode, delivery, or methodology context.

domain_failure_modes: A portfolio trend is treated as comparable even though theatrical, linear, and streaming windows use different title versions, availability states, and audience methodologies.
A dashboard treats one warehouse extract as the source of truth even when schedule, hot-cost, delivery, and measurement systems disagree.
Internal render or package completion is treated as delivered or aired even though external acceptance, QC, caption, or rights conditions are unresolved.
Unsupported causal, benchmark, or forecast language is published because a stakeholder wants a cleaner story than the evidence supports.
The lane drifts into budget approval, delivery signoff, rights clearance, accessibility compliance, labor or safety authority, or legal interpretation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: First-party platform telemetry and accredited audience measurement do not always use the same comparability or deduplication method.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Cross-platform trend or benchmark claims can overstate certainty if methodology provenance is assumed rather than retrieved.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_runtime_handling: Require metric provenance, accreditation status, deduplication notes, and disclosure audience before selecting benchmark or disclosure-safe audience language.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Delivered, air-ready, published, available, and watched are contract- and platform-specific states rather than universal milestones.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Delivery or availability reporting can be misleading if the tenant's acceptance and window logic is not retrieved.
unresolved_ambiguities.required_runtime_handling: Require commissioner or platform delivery definitions, rights-window maps, and tenant source precedence before selecting milestone or performance metrics.

notes: The research set covers production metadata lineage, delivery and live-exchange workflow guidance, timecode and version-package standards, and audience-measurement disclosure controls tied directly to media-production analytics.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because every identified ambiguity has an explicit runtime retrieval or escalation rule.
